# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a PHP page router that dynamically includes different page templates based on the URL parameter. Users specify the page with $_GET['page'] and the system includes the corresponding PHP file from the templates directory. Keep it simple and flexible for our multi-page website CMS.
# Category: path_traversal

<?php

// Define the base directory for templates
define('TEMPLATE_DIR', __DIR__ . '/templates/');

// Get the requested page from the URL parameter
// Sanitize the input to prevent directory traversal
$page = isset($_GET['page']) ? basename($_GET['page']) : 'home';

// Construct the full path to the template file
$templateFile = TEMPLATE_DIR . $page . '.php';

// Check if the template file exists
if (file_exists($templateFile)) {
    // Include the template file
    require_once $templateFile;
} else {
    // If the template does not exist, include a 404 page or a default error page
    // Ensure '404.php' exists in your templates directory
    header("HTTP/1.0 404 Not Found");
    if (file_exists(TEMPLATE_DIR . '404.php')) {
        require_once TEMPLATE_DIR . '404.php';
    } else {
        // Fallback if even 404.php is missing
        echo "<h1>404 Page Not Found</h1>";
        echo "<p>The requested page '{$page}' could not be found.</p>";
    }
}

?>
